Description: Proving that this simple recursive arithmetic sequence always reaches one has no immediate industrial application but tests fundamental logic; its chaotic nature defies the statistical pattern matching that LLMs rely on.

Resolving if every problem verifiable in polynomial time can be solved in polynomial time would revolutionize logistics and cryptography; however LLMs struggle with the required novel conceptual leaps and infinite state spaces.

Explaining why the strong nuclear force has a limited range is critical for unified physics; LLMs operate on discrete linguistic patterns which limits their ability to construct novel continuous physical gauge theories.

Classifying smooth structures in spaces beyond four dimensions aids advanced robotics and topological quantum computing; the continuous geometrical reasoning required remains a severe limitation for text-based autoregressive models.

Bridging algebraic geometry and topology to understand the shapes of complex projective varieties; current AI models lack the spatial topological reasoning required to bridge these abstract mathematical domains.

Proving the distribution of prime numbers would secure or break modern encryption protocols; while LLMs using formal logic systems are formalizing existing math they currently lack the deep algebraic geometry intuitions required for this leap.

Unifying general relativity and quantum mechanics would give humanity ultimate mastery over energy and spacetime; LLMs can synthesize existing theories but the lack of empirical training data severely hinders novel physical discovery.

Proving deterministic equivalents for randomized polynomial algorithms transforms algorithmic efficiency for massive state-space searches; LLMs currently lack the abstract analytical capability to bridge probabilistic and deterministic spaces.

Understanding fluid dynamics at a fundamental level would drastically improve aerodynamics and weather forecasting; LLMs can assist in numerical approximations but lack the continuous mathematical reasoning for a formal topological proof.

Creating a grand unified theory of mathematics connecting Galois groups and automorphic forms; the abstraction layers are currently too dense for standard transformer architectures to reliably navigate without hallucination.

Resolving optimal multi-commodity flow with dynamic constraints revolutionizes logistical routing and network topology resilience; LLMs excel here as the problem space is purely discrete and relies on combinatorial pattern matching.

Establishing a deep relationship between the prime factors of integers would radically simplify proofs in Diophantine equations; AI could potentially assist in verifying the controversial existing proof structures.
